# Request Network Docs

> Protocol for accepting and sending crypto payments at scale across EVM and Tron — instant payment reconciliation, click-to-pay payment links, cross-chain routing, and programmatic payouts. Products: [Dashboard](https://dashboard.request.network) (no-code), hosted [Secure Payment](https://pay.request.network) pages (`pay.request.network`), and the **Request Network REST API** (`https://api.request.network/v2`). Webhooks and Client IDs are managed via the **Auth API** (`https://auth.request.network/v1`). Full documentation index: https://docs.request.network

## Request Network API (what it is)

The **Request API** is a hosted REST layer over the Request Network payment protocol. You do not run nodes or deploy payment contracts yourself for the common integration path.

| Service | Base URL | Role |
| ------- | -------- | ---- |
| Request API | `https://api.request.network/v2` | Create secure payment links (`POST /secure-payments`), query requests/payments, payouts, payee destinations, currencies |
| Auth API | `https://auth.request.network/v1` | Register webhooks (`POST /webhook`), manage Client IDs (`POST /client-ids`) |
| Secure Payment (hosted UI) | `https://pay.request.network` | Payer-facing checkout: wallet connect, contract safety, optional KYT, cross-chain swap-to-pay (Li.Fi), signing |
| Dashboard | `https://dashboard.request.network` | Wallet sign-in, payment destination, generate Client ID, view requests |

**Recommended integration (happy path):** your backend calls `POST /v2/secure-payments` with a **Client ID**, receives `securePaymentUrl`, redirects the buyer to `pay.request.network`. The hosted page handles all web3 UX. You learn that funds settled via **webhooks** (`payment.confirmed`), not from the browser alone.

**Alternative (advanced):** embedded wallet flow — your frontend executes calldata from `/v2/request`, `/v2/payouts`, or secure-payment token endpoints. Same webhook reconciliation. See [Secure Payment Integration Guide](https://docs.request.network/api-features/secure-payment-integration-guide.md) and Optional section below.

OpenAPI: [openapi.v2.json](https://docs.request.network/api-reference/openapi.v2.json)

## User story (canonical merchant checkout)

**As a merchant,** I want a customer to pay an order amount in crypto/stablecoin from any supported wallet and chain, **so that** I receive funds on my configured chain/token and my app marks the order paid reliably.

**Actors:** merchant backend, payer browser, Request API, hosted Secure Payment page, Auth API webhooks.

**Flow:**

1. Customer clicks Pay on the merchant site.
2. Merchant backend creates a secure payment link (amount + merchant `reference` such as order ID).
3. Customer is redirected to `pay.request.network`, pays on-chain.
4. Request Network sends `payment.confirmed` to the merchant webhook URL.
5. Merchant fulfills the order (ship, unlock SaaS, etc.).
6. Customer may click a “back to store” button on the success screen (`redirectUrl`) — this is UX only, not proof of payment.

**Audience:** e-commerce, marketplaces, SaaS billing, invoicing products. Tutorial: [Integration Tutorial](https://docs.request.network/api-setup/integration-tutorial.md), [Programmatic payment links](https://docs.request.network/use-cases/programmatic-payment-links.md), [Quickstart](https://docs.request.network/use-cases/quickstart.md).

## Credentials you actually need

For a typical **server-side hosted checkout**, store only these secrets in environment variables (never in git):

| Variable | What it is | Where to get it |
| -------- | ---------- | --------------- |
| `RN_CLIENT_ID` | Primary credential; sent as header `x-client-id` on API and webhook registration | [Dashboard](https://dashboard.request.network): sign in with wallet → **create a payment destination first** (chain + token) → Manage Destination → **Client IDs** → Generate. Or `POST /v1/client-ids` on Auth API with a wallet session. |
| `RN_WEBHOOK_SECRET` | HMAC signing secret for inbound webhooks | Returned **once** when you `POST https://auth.request.network/v1/webhook` with `x-client-id` and `{ "url": "https://yoursite.com/webhooks/request-network" }`. Cannot be retrieved later; rotate by creating a new webhook if lost. |

Also configure (not always secret):

- **Payment destination** — where you receive funds. Created in the Dashboard (Step 2 of [Quickstart](https://docs.request.network/use-cases/quickstart.md)). When the Client ID is **bound** to that destination, you usually **omit** `destinationId` in `POST /secure-payments`.
- **`destinationId`** (optional in API body) — ERC-7828 composite `{interopAddress}:{tokenAddress}`; required only for multi-destination or explicit routing. Shown in Dashboard after destination creation.

**Optional — not required for happy path:**

- **`x-api-key`** — separate server-only secret for trusted backends. **Never expose to the browser.** Most integrations use **Client ID only** on the server (`x-client-id` without `Origin`). See [Authentication](https://docs.request.network/api-reference/authentication.md).

```bash
# .env (minimal hosted checkout)
RN_API_BASE=https://api.request.network/v2
RN_AUTH_BASE=https://auth.request.network/v1
RN_CLIENT_ID=your_client_id_from_dashboard
RN_WEBHOOK_SECRET=from_webhook_registration_response_once
```

**Dashboard ordering gotcha:** the Client IDs section appears only **after** a payment destination exists.

## Security — credential disclosure

- **Create payment links and register webhooks only from your server** (or serverless backend). Put `RN_CLIENT_ID` and `RN_WEBHOOK_SECRET` in server environment variables.
- **Do not** embed `RN_WEBHOOK_SECRET` in frontend code, mobile apps, or public repos. Webhook verification must run server-side.
- **Client ID in the browser** is supported only when intentional: use Dashboard **allowed domains** so `x-client-id` + browser `Origin` are whitelisted. For standard “redirect to pay.request.network” checkout, keep Client ID **server-side only** — the payer never needs your Client ID.
- **Never** put an **API key** (`x-api-key`) in client-side code.
- **Webhooks:** verify `x-request-network-signature` against the **raw** request body with `RN_WEBHOOK_SECRET` before parsing JSON; use timing-safe compare. Treat payer landing on a “thank you” page as a **hint**; fulfill on `payment.confirmed` only.
- Production webhook URLs must use **HTTPS**. Respond 2xx within ~5s; do heavy work asynchronously; dedupe on `x-request-network-delivery` or `requestId`.

## Important warnings and breaking behaviors

Read these before shipping — they are common integration failures, not edge cases.

1. **No auto-redirect after payment.** `redirectUrl` / `redirectLabel` only render a button on the Secure Payment success screen. Reconciliation is **webhooks** (or explicit status polling), not the redirect.
2. **`amount` is a human-readable string** (e.g. `"25.00"`), not wei. The API applies token decimals.
3. **Webhook secret is shown once** at `POST /v1/webhook`. Store it immediately as `RN_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.
4. **Duplicate webhook events.** Scoped events (`payment.confirmed.checkout`, `payment.confirmed.client_id`, etc.) fire **in addition to** `payment.confirmed`. Dedupe before fulfilling.
5. **Batch secure payments are EVM-only.** Multiple `requests[]` on Tron returns 400 (“Batch payments are not supported for TRON networks”).
6. **Cross-chain swap-to-pay** on the hosted page is EVM-source oriented (USDC/USDT routes via Li.Fi); see [Crosschain Payments](https://docs.request.network/api-features/crosschain-payments.md).
7. **`feePercentage` requires `feeAddress`** (both or neither). Same for `redirectLabel` requiring `redirectUrl`.
8. **Wallet Dashboard session** expires after ~15 minutes idle; re-sign if Auth/Dashboard calls fail mid-setup.
9. Use **v2** API paths (`/v2/...`). Prefer this doc index and [Endpoints Overview](https://docs.request.network/api-reference/endpoints-overview.md) over legacy SDK-only flows unless you explicitly need the SDK.

## Happy path integration (hosted Secure Payment)

Implement these three pieces on your stack (Node, Next.js, Django, Rails, PHP, serverless — same pattern).

### 0. Prerequisites (one-time)

Complete [Quickstart](https://docs.request.network/use-cases/quickstart.md) steps 1–4: wallet sign-in → payment destination → Client ID → webhook URL registered.

### 1. Payment link creation (server)

`POST /v2/secure-payments` with `x-client-id`. Persist `reference`, `requestIds`, and `token` before redirecting.

```javascript
// Your backend route (e.g. POST /checkout)
const res = await fetch(`${process.env.RN_API_BASE}/secure-payments`, {
  method: "POST",
  headers: {
    "x-client-id": process.env.RN_CLIENT_ID,
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    requests: [{ amount: "25.00" }],           // string, human-readable
    reference: "ORDER-2026-001",               // your order id for reconciliation
    redirectUrl: "https://yoursite.com/orders/ORDER-2026-001/thank-you",
    redirectLabel: "Back to store",
    // destinationId: optional if Client ID is bound to one payee destination
  }),
});
if (!res.ok) throw new Error(await res.text());
const { securePaymentUrl, requestIds, token } = await res.json();
// Save { reference, requestIds, token, status: "awaiting_payment" } in your DB
// Redirect buyer: res.redirect(302, securePaymentUrl) or return { redirectUrl: securePaymentUrl } to frontend
```

Response shape: `{ "requestIds": ["..."], "securePaymentUrl": "https://pay.request.network/?token=...", "token": "..." }`.

Docs: [Secure Payments API](https://docs.request.network/api-reference/secure-payments.md), [Secure Payment Pages](https://docs.request.network/api-features/secure-payment-pages.md).

### 2. Webhook URL (server)

Register (once per Client ID scope):

```bash
curl -X POST "$RN_AUTH_BASE/webhook" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "x-client-id: $RN_CLIENT_ID" \
  -d '{ "url": "https://yoursite.com/webhooks/request-network" }'
# => { "id": "...", "secret": "STORE_AS_RN_WEBHOOK_SECRET" }
```

Handler essentials (Express-style): raw body → verify signature → parse → on `payment.confirmed` mark order paid by `reference` / `requestId`.

```javascript
import crypto from "node:crypto";
import express from "express";

function verify(rawBody, signature, secret) {
  const expected = crypto.createHmac("sha256", secret).update(rawBody).digest("hex");
  try {
    return crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(signature), Buffer.from(expected));
  } catch { return false; }
}

// Mount with express.raw({ type: "application/json", verify: (req, _res, buf) => { req.rawBody = buf; } })
app.post("/webhooks/request-network", async (req, res) => {
  const sig = req.headers["x-request-network-signature"];
  if (!verify(req.rawBody, sig, process.env.RN_WEBHOOK_SECRET))
    return res.status(401).end();

  const body = JSON.parse(req.rawBody.toString("utf8"));
  res.status(200).json({ ok: true }); // ack fast

  if (body.event === "payment.confirmed" || body.event === "payment.confirmed.checkout") {
    // await fulfillOrder(body.requestId, body.reference, body.txHash, ...);
  }
});
```

Test delivery: `POST /v1/webhook/test` with `{ "eventType": "payment.confirmed" }` and `x-client-id`. Docs: [Webhooks](https://docs.request.network/api-reference/webhooks.md), [Webhooks & Events](https://docs.request.network/api-features/webhooks-events.md).

### 3. “Thank you” page (merchant frontend)

- Set `redirectUrl` when creating the secure payment (see step 1) to your order confirmation route, e.g. `https://yoursite.com/orders/{orderId}/thank-you`.
- **UI copy:** explain payment may still be confirming on-chain; show order as **paid** only after your backend processed the webhook (or show “pending” and poll your own order API).
- **Do not** grant entitlements solely because the user opened the thank-you page or clicked “Back to store” on `pay.request.network`.

Minimal frontend after your backend returns `securePaymentUrl`:

```javascript
const { redirectUrl } = await fetch("/api/checkout", { method: "POST", body: JSON.stringify({ amount, orderId }) }).then(r => r.json());
window.location.href = redirectUrl; // pay.request.network
```

---

## Optional — beyond the happy path

### Alternative integration paths

- **Embedded checkout** — wallet connect, approvals, and tx submission in your UI: [Secure Payment Integration Guide](https://docs.request.network/api-features/secure-payment-integration-guide.md), calldata via request/pay endpoints.
- **Partial payments** — [Partial Payments](https://docs.request.network/api-features/partial-payments.md); handle `payment.partial` webhooks.
- **Failed / refunded flows** — `payment.failed`, `payment.refunded` events; [Lifecycle of a Request](https://docs.request.network/resources/lifecycle-of-a-request.md).
- **Polling instead of webhooks** — possible via [Get request status](https://docs.request.network/api-reference/request/get-request-status.md) / [Search payments](https://docs.request.network/api-reference/v2payments/search-payments-with-advanced-filtering.md); webhooks remain recommended ([Webhook reconciliation](https://docs.request.network/use-cases/webhook-reconciliation.md)).
- **No-code links** — Dashboard-only: [No-code payment links](https://docs.request.network/use-cases/no-code-payment-links.md).
- **Compliance-gated payers (KYT)** — [Compliance-gated payments](https://docs.request.network/use-cases/compliance-gated-payments.md).
- **Conversion, crypto-to-fiat, recurring, commerce (authorize/capture)** — see API Features links below.

### Advanced mechanisms

- **Batch payouts (EVM)** — many recipients, one signed tx: [Batch payouts use case](https://docs.request.network/use-cases/batch-payouts.md), [Batch Payments](https://docs.request.network/api-features/batch-payments.md), [Pay multiple requests in one transaction](https://docs.request.network/api-reference/pay/pay-multiple-requests-in-one-transaction.md).
- **Programmatic payouts** — [Payouts](https://docs.request.network/api-features/payouts.md), hosted outgoing links `POST /v2/secure-payments/payouts`.
- **Multi-chain checkout / Li.Fi routing** — [Multi-chain checkout](https://docs.request.network/use-cases/multi-chain-checkout.md), [Crosschain Payments](https://docs.request.network/api-features/crosschain-payments.md).
- **Payee destinations & orchestrator Client IDs** — [Payee Destinations](https://docs.request.network/api-features/payee-destinations.md), [Client ID Management](https://docs.request.network/api-features/client-id-management.md).
- **Platform and protocol fees** — [Platform Fees](https://docs.request.network/api-features/platform-fees.md), [Protocol Fees](https://docs.request.network/api-features/protocol-fees.md), [Fee Breakdowns](https://docs.request.network/api-features/fee-breakdowns.md).
